issueDetails.example:
"The endpoint appears to be vulnerable to Server Side Request Forgery attack. The original request was replayed by replacing CSV upload parameter with url that redirects to AWS IMDS endpoint. The application responded with 2XX success code and also gave out sensitive AWS information in response. Server-Side Request Forgery (SSRF) flaws occur whenever an API is fetching a remote resource without validating the user-supplied URL. It allows an attacker to coerce the application to send a crafted request to an unexpected destination, even when protected by a firewall or a VPN.
 "Modern concepts in application development make SSRF more common and more dangerous.
" "More common - the following concepts encourage developers to access an external resource based on user input: Webhooks, file fetching from URLs, custom SSO, and URL previews.
" "More dangerous - Modern technologies like cloud providers, Kubernetes, and Docker expose management and control channels over HTTP on predictable, well-known paths. Those channels are an easy target for an SSRF attack.
" "It is also more challenging to limit outbound traffic from your application, because of the connected nature of modern applications.
" + "The SSRF risk can not always be completely eliminated. While choosing a protection mechanism, it is important to consider the business risks and needs."

issueDescription.example: Quite often, APIs do not impose any restrictions on the size or number of resources that can be requested by the user.

testName.example: BFLA by HTTP DELETE method overriding

/api/createCustomRole.description: Creates a new custom role with specified base role, collection access, and feature permissions.

roleName.description: Unique name for the role. Max 50 characters. Alphanumeric, hyphens, and underscores only.

baseRole.description: Base role from which this custom role inherits. One of: ADMIN, MEMBER, GUEST.

apiCollectionIds.description: List of API collection IDs this role has access to.

defaultInviteRole.description: Whether this role is the default role assigned to new invites.

allowedFeaturesForUser.description: List of feature keys enabled for users in this role.

/api/updateCustomRole.description: Updates an existing custom role's base role, collection access, and feature permissions.

apiCollectionIds.description: Updated list of API collection IDs this role has access to.

defaultInviteRole.description: Whether this role should become the default role for new invites.

allowedFeaturesForUser.description: Updated list of feature keys enabled for users in this role.

/api/deleteCustomRole.description: Deletes a custom role by name. The role must not be assigned to any users or pending invites.

scopeRoleMapping.description: Map of product scope to role. Valid scopes include API, ENDPOINT, etc. Use NO_ACCESS to revoke access for a scope.

200.description: Scope-role mapping updated and user cache cleared.

schema.example: YOUR_API_KEY from Settings > Integrations > Akto APIs

Empty response body; processing happens asynchronously.

apiTokenList.description: List containing the newly created ApiToken object.

authParamData.description: List of auth parameter entries specifying where and how to inject credentials.

requestData.description: HTTP request steps used to obtain credentials (for LOGIN_REQUEST type).

schema.example: YOUR_API_KEY from Settings > Integrations > Akto APIs

/api/triggerLoginSteps.description: Executes login flow steps and returns step-by-step responses. Used to validate login sequences before saving an auth mechanism.

authParamData.description: Auth parameters extracted from login flow responses.

requestData.description: Ordered list of HTTP requests forming the login flow.

miniTestingServiceName.description: Optional name of mini-testing service to execute the login steps.

200.description: Returns responses for each login flow step.

responses.description: Step-by-step HTTP responses from executing the login flow.

schema.example: YOUR_API_KEY from Settings > Integrations > Akto APIs

/api/createTestSuite.description: Creates a new custom test suite with a given name and list of test subcategories.

testSuiteName.description: Display name for the new test suite. A numeric suffix is appended if the name already exists.

subCategoryList.description: List of test subcategory identifiers to include in this suite.

schema.example: YOUR_API_KEY from Settings > Integrations > Akto APIs

/api/modifyTestSuite.description: Updates an existing custom test suite's name and/or subcategories. Only the creator or an ADMIN can modify a suite.

testSuiteHexId.description: Hex-encoded ObjectId of the test suite to modify.

testSuiteName.description: New display name for the test suite.

subCategoryList.description: Replacement list of test subcategory identifiers.

schema.example: YOUR_API_KEY from Settings > Integrations > Akto APIs

/api/deleteTestSuite.description: Deletes a custom test suite by its hex ID.

testSuiteHexId.description: Hex-encoded ObjectId of the test suite to delete.

schema.example: YOUR_API_KEY from Settings > Integrations > Akto APIs

/api/bulkUpdateTestResultsSeverity.description: Bulk-updates the severity level of selected test results across all related collections.

testingRunResultHexIds.description: Hex IDs of the test results to update.

severityToBeUpdated.description: New severity level. One of: CRITICAL, HIGH, MEDIUM, LOW.

200.description: Severity updated across all related collections.

schema.example: YOUR_API_KEY from Settings > Integrations > Akto APIs

/api/deleteTestRuns.description: Deletes the specified test runs and schedules a background job to clean up associated results and summaries.

testRunIds.description: Hex-encoded ObjectIds of the test runs to delete.

200.description: Test runs deleted and cleanup job enqueued.

schema.example: YOUR_API_KEY from Settings > Integrations > Akto APIs

/api/deleteTestRunsFromSummaries.description: Deletes test data (results and summaries) identified by testing run result summary IDs via a background job.

latestSummaryIds.description: Hex-encoded ObjectIds of the testing run result summaries to delete.

200.description: Deletion job enqueued for the specified summaries.

schema.example: YOUR_API_KEY from Settings > Integrations > Akto APIs

/api/changeStatus.description: Changes the active/inactive status of a custom webhook and updates its associated scheduled job.

id.description: ID of the custom webhook to update.

activeStatus.description: New status. One of: ACTIVE, INACTIVE.

schema.example: YOUR_API_KEY from Settings > Integrations > Akto APIs

/api/runOnce.description: Triggers an immediate one-time asynchronous execution of a custom webhook.

id.description: ID of the custom webhook to execute.

schema.description: Empty response body; execution happens asynchronously.

schema.example: YOUR_API_KEY from Settings > Integrations > Akto APIs

/api/runTestOnboarding.description: Runs the onboarding test suite against a specified collection with provided auth credentials, creating the auth mechanism and test run, then marks onboarding as complete.

testSuite.description: Test suite enum value to run during onboarding.

collectionId.description: ID of the API collection to test.

authParamData.description: Authentication parameters for the onboarding test.

testingRunHexId.description: Hex ID of the created onboarding testing run.

schema.example: YOUR_API_KEY from Settings > Integrations > Akto APIs

/api/saveDefaultPayload.description: Saves or updates a default payload pattern for a domain, recording the creator and timestamps.

domain.description: Domain name to associate the payload with.

pattern.description: JSON or text payload pattern to save as the default for this domain.

200.description: Payload saved. Returns the updated DefaultPayload object.

schema.example: YOUR_API_KEY from Settings > Integrations > Akto APIs

/api/saveReplaceDetails.description: Saves key-value replacement pairs for a specific API endpoint, used in dependency flow testing.

apiCollectionId.description: ID of the API collection containing the endpoint.

url.description: URL path of the API endpoint.

kvPairs.description: Key-value pairs specifying parameter replacements for this endpoint.

schema.example: YOUR_API_KEY from Settings > Integrations > Akto APIs

/api/saveGlobalVars.description: Saves global host variable mappings for dependency flow testing (current hostname to replacement hostname).

200.description: Global host variable mappings saved successfully.

schema.example: YOUR_API_KEY from Settings > Integrations > Akto APIs

/api/invokeDependencyTable.description: Asynchronously invokes the dependency graph build job for the specified collections, creating a temporary collection to store the dependency flow data.

apiCollectionIds.description: Collection IDs to invoke the dependency graph for.

sourceCodeApis.description: If true, APIs are from source code analysis and the original collection is used instead of creating a temp one.

newCollectionId.description: ID of the temporary collection created to hold dependency flow data.

schema.example: YOUR_API_KEY from Settings > Integrations > Akto APIs

/api/getMcpServersByAgent.description: Retrieves MCP servers configured for a specific agent or device, including server name, URL, and last seen timestamp.

agentId.description: Optional agent ID to filter MCP servers by.

deviceId.description: Optional device ID to filter MCP servers by.

200.description: Returns MCP servers for the specified agent/device.

schema.example: YOUR_API_KEY from Settings > Integrations > Akto APIs

/api/getAgentLogs.description: Fetches agent logs for a specific agent within a time range, sorted by timestamp (newest first), capped at 10,000 logs.

agentId.description: Required agent ID to fetch logs for.

startTime.description: Optional start timestamp in seconds (defaults to 24 hours ago).

endTime.description: Optional end timestamp in seconds (defaults to current time).

200.description: Returns agent logs for the specified time range.

agentLogs.description: List of agent log entries, sorted by timestamp (newest first).

schema.example: YOUR_API_KEY from Settings > Integrations > Akto APIs

/api/getMcpdata.description: Fetches MCP-related metrics and data based on filter type, including API counts, audit alerts, and traffic analysis.

filterType.description: Type of filter. One of: TOTAL_APIS, NEW_APIS_7_DAYS, THIRD_PARTY_APIS, RECENT_OPEN_ALERTS, CRITICAL_APIS, TOOLS, PROMPTS, RESOURCES, MCP_SERVER, TOP_3_APPLICATIONS_BY_TRAFFIC, POLICY_GUARDRAIL_APIS.

200.description: Returns MCP data matching the filter type.

mcpDataCount.description: Count of data matching the filter type.

auditAlerts.description: List of McpAuditInfo (only for RECENT_OPEN_ALERTS filter).

response.description: Additional response data (alertDetails or topApplications depending on filter type).

schema.example: YOUR_API_KEY from Settings > Integrations > Akto APIs

/api/addMcpRegistryIntegration.description: Adds or updates MCP registry integrations with validation for duplicate names/URLs and maximum limits (10 registries max).

registries.description: List of MCP registries to add or update.

200.description: Registry integrations added. Returns the current configuration.

mcpRegistryConfig.description: Current MCP registry configuration with all registries.

schema.example: YOUR_API_KEY from Settings > Integrations > Akto APIs

/api/initiateMCPScan.description: Initiates an MCP server scan by creating an API collection and scheduling a MCP tools sync job.

serverUrl.description: Required MCP server URL to scan.

dashboardUrl.description: Dashboard URL for callbacks and result reporting.

200.description: MCP scan initiated. Returns the created or updated API collection.

createdCollection.description: The created or updated ApiCollection for the MCP server.

schema.example: YOUR_API_KEY from Settings > Integrations > Akto APIs

/api/initiateMCPRecon.description: Initiates MCP reconnaissance for specified IP ranges, creating a pending reconnaissance request.

ipRange.description: Required IP range(s) to perform reconnaissance on. Can be single IP or CIDR notation.

schema.example: YOUR_API_KEY from Settings > Integrations > Akto APIs

/api/fetchMcpToolsApiCalls.description: Fetches API calls made by MCP tools in a collection, grouping them by parent tool names.

apiCollectionId.description: API collection ID containing MCP tools.

200.description: Returns tool API calls grouped by tool.

response.description: Map of tool name to list of API calls made by that tool.

schema.example: YOUR_API_KEY from Settings > Integrations > Akto APIs

/api/fetchMcpAuditInfoByCollection.description: Fetches MCP audit information for a specific API collection by extracting the MCP server name from hostname.

apiCollectionId.description: API collection ID to fetch audit info for.

200.description: Returns MCP audit information for the collection.

schema.example: YOUR_API_KEY from Settings > Integrations > Akto APIs

/api/createAgentRun.description: Creates a new agent run with initialization data, checking for existing running agents and validating the model.

modelName.description: Optional AI model name to use. Defaults to AKTO_AGENT_MODEL.

githubAccessToken.description: Optional GitHub token for source code agents.

agentRun.description: Updated AgentRun object with new state and timestamps.

schema.example: YOUR_API_KEY from Settings > Integrations > Akto APIs

schema.example: YOUR_API_KEY from Settings > Integrations > Akto APIs
